useful_deltas.summary: A predeclared prior-bar hv_20/hv_60>=1.20 plus non-positive-return CCS failed all 24 expanding-origin train gates across eight symbols under dual proxy costs and hard path-risk gates; the family is closed without tuning, registration, or promotion.

critic_findings.finding: The CCS module lacked a local passing-holdout/failing-train negative control, and BUILD direction-bias wording under-mentioned the four prior PCS signal rejects.
critic_findings.rationale: The local CCS suite now proves that a passing holdout cannot rescue a failed train cost axis. BUILD and generated coverage wording now name close-shock, momentum, pullback, and volatility-compression PCS alongside the new volatility-expansion CCS rejection.

critic_findings.finding: Sparse samples imply the family should be called inconclusive rather than rejected.
critic_findings.rationale: The predeclared falsifier required at least eight trades on every train and holdout cost axis; sparse and zero samples therefore fail closed. The claim is rejection on this synthetic evidence, not proof that no real-world CCS edge can exist.

critic_findings.finding: The executor should tune the volatility threshold, DTE, or management after zero of 24 train gates passed.
critic_findings.rationale: Tuning the same synthetic daily-bar evidence after universal train-gate failure would be holdout-adjacent search and thrash. The family is closed until a genuinely new evidence class or repaired capability exists.

critic_findings.finding: The prior archive NEXT should have been executed on Sunday instead of superseded.
critic_findings.rationale: Archive density can advance only on a distinct New York RTH market date; Sunday supplied no valid new date. The distinct-RTH append remains the sole NEXT seed.

data_dependencies: A distinct New York RTH market date is required to advance observed TSLL option archive density from 1/3 to 2/3.
Provider-backed historical simulation, observed-cost calibration, evolve-on-observed-grid, and L1 claims remain blocked until at least 3/3 distinct market dates.
next: On the next distinct New York RTH market date, append one all-expiration TSLL option observation snapshot and verify archive density advances from 1/3 to 2/3 without duplicating identical rows; do not run provider-backed historical simulation, observed-cost calibration, evolve-on-observed-grid, or L1 claims before 3/3.
